reasoning: While programmatic signals show high content recall (98.7%), the LLM fidelity evaluation identifies three major structural defects that compromise document accessibility and flow. The most significant issue is the disruption of reading order, where header logos were injected into the middle of a functional list. These structural and navigational failures, combined with a low structural fidelity score of 0.45, require human intervention to ensure the document meets accessibility standards.

concerns.analysis: The heading hierarchy is inconsistent. On the first page, the primary section headings 'State agency cooperation...' and 'When to Report to the State' are assigned level 3 (H3). However, starting on page 2, equivalent primary section headings such as 'What to Report', 'How to Report...', 'Types of State Incident Response', and 'Mission-Critical Support' are assigned level 2 (H2). This inconsistency creates a broken navigational structure for screen reader users as equivalent sections are nested
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The headings 'State agency cooperation...', 'When to Report to the State', 'What to Report', etc., are all styled identically as primary section headers (bold, underlined).
concerns.output_quote: Reading Order 4 and 9 are Heading Level 3, whereas Reading Order 19, 23, 33, and 36 are Heading Level 2.
concerns.location: sections[0].elements[3], sections[0].elements[8], sections[1].elements[3], sections[1].elements[7], sections[2].elements[2], sections[2].elements[5]

concerns.analysis: The reading order is significantly disrupted by the placement of the document's header logos. In the source PDF, the NCDIT, State Seal, and DPS logos are at the very top of the document. In the JSON output, these images (Reading Orders 13, 14, 15) are placed at the end of the content for the first page, appearing after the 'When to Report' section and the start of its associated list. This moves decorative/header elements into the middle of the instructional text flow.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The logos appear at the top of Page 1, above the document title.
concerns.output_quote: The images (Logos) are assigned reading_order 13, 14, and 15, which places them after the first half of the 'When to Report' section.

concerns.analysis: In the source PDF, the agency logos (NCDIT, North Carolina State Seal, and DPS) are positioned at the top of the first page as a document header. In the HTML output, these logos have been moved and inserted into the middle of the 'When to Report to the State' section. They appear between the second and third bullet points of a list that was originally contiguous in the source document (spanning the bottom of page 1 and the top of page 2). This rearrangement of header elements into the body of th
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Logos at top of Page 1; Bulleted list 'Pursuant to N.C.G.S. 143B-1379...' spanning pages 1 and 2.
concerns.output_quote: The DPS, NCDIT, and State Seal logos are inserted between the bullet point 'Have an impact on a large number of victims' and the bullet point 'Indicate unauthorized access to, or malicious software present on...'
concerns.location: Section 'When to Report to the State'

concerns.analysis: The table 'Reporting Points of Contact' consists of two columns. In the output JSON, the final row (Reading Order 25, row index 4) contains only a single cell ('Contact the Enterprise Security...') which spans only the first column implicitly. In the source document, this content belongs to the left column while the right column is empty. By providing only one cell in a two-column row without a defined span, the structural integrity of the table is compromised.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The table 'Reporting Points of Contact' on Page 2 has a consistent two-column layout.
concerns.output_quote: The last row of the table (reading_order 25) contains only one cell: "Contact the Enterprise Security and Risk Management Office at mailto:DITThreatManagement@nc.gov."

concerns.analysis: The bulleted list under 'When to Report to the State' consists of 5 items spanning the page break between pages 1 and 2. The pipeline has split this into two separate list objects (Reading Order 12 containing 2 items and Reading Order 16 containing 3 items). This fails to preserve the semantic integrity of the single list found in the source.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: A single bulleted list of 5 items describing cyber incidents that must be reported.
concerns.output_quote: The list is divided into two separate list elements at reading_order 12 and reading_order 16.

action_items: Relocate NCDIT/DPS logos from the body text to the document header.
Fix heading levels to be consistent (e.g., all primary sections as H2).
Merge the split bulleted list under 'When to Report to the State' into a single semantic list.
Correct the final row of the 'Reporting Points of Contact' table to maintain two-column alignment.
